event: diagnosed with a palpable mass on her right breast
pathological examination confirmed an invasive ductal carcinoma
No lymphocytic infiltration was detected on the pre-treatment sample
a staging CT scan of the thorax and abdomen was negative for distant metastasis
neoadjuvant chemotherapy with paclitaxel 80 mg/m2 plus carboplatin AUC2 d1-8-15 q28 for 4 cycles was planned
no anthracyclines due to previous exposure
no hematological toxicity of any grade
skin overlying PORT showed signs of subcutaneous cellulitis
broad-spectrum i.v. antibiotic therapy with piperacillin/tazobactam and daptomycin was started
peripheral blood cultures negative for infection
i.v. catheter tip showed positivity for Klebsiella pneumoniae
sensitive to both meropenem and levofloxacin
transferred to the Thoracic Surgery Unit
left thoracoscopy with pleural and mediastinal drainage
admitted to the Intensive Care Unit for observation and support
intensive inflammatory infiltrate including mainly neutrophils
CT scan showed progressive resolution of mediastinitis
Wound improvement with scar was evident
no change in the dimension of the lump
Breast surgical wound healing was regular
isolated neoplastic cells for maximum 2 mm extension
a moderate infiltrate was present in the surgical sample
a restaging brain/chest/abdomen CT was negative for distant metastasis
resumed chemotherapy with carboplatin and paclitaxel with dose reduction
completed her fourth and last cycle
